steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Both sentences describe what guard cells help regulate (carbon dioxide intake, then water loss). The second adds another role to the first.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is addition. Both sentences describe separate roles that guard cells perform: regulating carbon dioxide intake, then regulating water loss. Neither sentence qualifies or contradicts the other — the second simply adds an equal-weight function.
  • Choice A — "In conclusion,": signals a final summary that wraps up prior discussion. This is not a concluding statement — it is just adding another function. ✗
  • Choice B — "Additionally,": signals an added piece of information of equal weight, fitting perfectly since regulating water loss is a second separate role alongside regulating CO2. ✓
  • Choice C — "Instead,": signals that the second sentence replaces or contradicts the first. Guard cells do both things — regulating water loss is not a replacement for regulating CO2. ✗
  • Choice D — "Previously,": signals a time shift to an earlier period. No time contrast is being drawn here. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
